mission: Publish the canonical Phase 0 runtime-topology contract as machine-readable law so workload families, trust-zone boundaries, failure domains, egress posture, and runtime publication placeholders become explicit inputs for later gateway, release, and frontend tasks.

trust_zones.purpose: TLS termination, origin allowlisting, request shaping, and zero-trust browser pre-filtering.
trust_zones.notes: This is the first browser-contacting band, but it is not the published route-compute boundary.

trust_zones.purpose: Static shell, publication bundle, and design/runtime parity delivery only.
trust_zones.notes: Shell assets stay exact to the promoted runtime bundle and may not become a hidden BFF.

trust_zones.purpose: The only browser-addressable compute boundary for published gateway surfaces.
primary_controls: No direct data-plane or adapter access
trust_zones.notes: Later seq_047 refines exact gateway splits, but seq_046 already freezes the zone and bridge law.

trust_zones.purpose: Canonical command and projection runtime planes with no direct browser reachability.
trust_zones.notes: Command and projection stay separate workload families even when they deploy from the same repository.

trust_zones.purpose: External effect dispatch, provider callback ingress, and simulator seams.
trust_zones.notes: Live-provider dispatch and simulator backplanes stay in the same trust zone but split at the workload-family level.

trust_zones.purpose: Relational, projection, queue, cache, object, and immutable store classes with explicit restore law.
primary_controls: Tenant tuple on every key or stronger scope tuple
trust_zones.notes: Queues and caches are stateful runtime concerns, not hidden details inside app or worker config.

trust_zones.purpose: Assurance slices, release/watch tuple publication, audit, resilience, and KMS-bound control paths.
trust_zones.notes: Support, operations, governance, and release controls consume one assurance plane instead of inventing local truth.

trust_zone_boundaries.notes: Static delivery remains bundle-bound and may not become a mutable runtime hop.
This is the only browser-to-compute bridge beyond the edge.
Gateway surfaces may read projections and submit commands only through declared bridge placeholders.
Support replay, operations drilldown, and governance shells may query assurance slices without bypassing the control plane.
Data-plane access is internal-only and tuple-bound.
Command handoff into integration always passes through durable queues or simulator contracts.
Mutation and projection planes both depend on exact assurance slices before live widening.
Provider outcomes settle through durable state before they can affect user-visible posture.
Integration failures widen only through explicit assurance tuples and watch publication.
Assurance is the only plane allowed to publish restore and release tuple verdicts into stateful stores.

workload_family_catalog.notes: Edge routing is shared but intentionally narrow: no PHI assembly and no direct adapter or data egress.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Split from the published gateway because static publication, release freeze linkage, and no-runtime-egress posture differ materially from browser-addressable compute.
workload_family_catalog.notes: Shell delivery stays exact to the RuntimePublicationBundle and cannot hide mutation or session logic.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Separated from static shell delivery because differing session policy, tenant isolation, bridge authority, and release-freeze posture would make one combined family unsafe.
workload_family_catalog.notes: This is the only family allowed to bridge browser traffic into command, projection, or assurance planes.
Canonical writes, blockers, settlements, and route-intent enforcement stay here and nowhere in the shell layer.
Projection is audience-safe derived truth only; it never upgrades canonical write authority.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Split from simulator work because live-provider dispatch has materially different egress, approval, and receipt-settlement posture than the local proof twin backplane.
workload_family_catalog.notes: All live-provider motion stays in durable queues and declared effect contracts; direct browser or data-plane reach is forbidden.

workload_family_catalog.split_rationale: Non-authoritative simulator seams stay out of preprod and production because proof-twin behavior must never be misread as live provider confirmation.
workload_family_catalog.notes: The simulator backplane is allowed only in nonprod rings and only as a declared proof twin or fallback seam.
Relational, queue, object, cache, and audit stores are explicit runtime concerns with restore law, not hidden infrastructure folklore.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9F. Resilience architecture, restore orchestration, and chaos programme
workload_family_catalog.notes: Assurance, security, governance, operations, and release consume one exact tuple-publishing control plane.
